strengths: Nearly two decades of engineering experience spanning web, mobile, embedded, and distributed systems, with current hands-on agentic-AI infrastructure work at re:cinq building MCP code generators and frameworks supporting agentic workflows — directly relevant to the role's agentic architecture mandate.

Proven high-scale distributed-systems depth: at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic he built low-latency microservices handling millions of HTTP requests per second using async I/O, caching, and real-time metadata stores, matching the event-streaming and real-time inference demands of the platform.

Meaningful fintech and product-ownership credentials: shipped investment-platform software at RIDE Capital with TDD/CI/CD discipline, optimized multi-country pricing systems at Optilyz, and founded/operates an event-driven, Kubernetes-deployed geo-data platform — evidence of architectural ownership and production reliability culture.
concerns: Career trajectory is individual-contributor (Senior/Staff) with no documented experience scaling engineering teams from 10 to 30+, developing a technical-leadership layer, or holding executive responsibility.
Agentic-AI experience is recent and tooling-oriented; the resume shows no production multi-agent systems operated in regulated or high-stakes commercial environments.
No demonstrated depth in credit-risk systems, payment flows, or governance of hybrid/outsourced engineering models, and no stated LLM selection, fine-tuning, or evaluation methodology experience.

key_factors.factor: Current hands-on agentic-AI and MCP tooling work
Fintech domain exposure and craftsmanship-driven delivery culture
No executive or team-scaling leadership track record

response.justification: This candidate offers a rare combination the role's technical core demands: genuine low-latency distributed-systems experience at millions-of-requests-per-second scale, current daily work on agentic-AI infrastructure including MCP code generation and agent-framework development, and direct fintech delivery at a German investment scale-up. His founder experience running an event-driven platform end-to-end, coupled with a long record of TDD, CI/CD, mentoring, and workshop leadership, maps well onto the role's emphasis on evaluation rigor, observability, and engineering culture. While his leadership experience is technical rather than organizational, his breadth, production-scale credentials, and hands-on agentic work make him a substantive candidate whose architecture-side fit is strong.
